description: No files modified outside the keyboard's own folder
Keyboard folder lives under `release/` or `experimental/`
Keyboard sits under an alphabetic or org-code organizing subfolder, not at the root of `release/`/`experimental/`
No source files placed at the repository root
The keyboard ID (folder name) is not already used in `experimental/`, `legacy/`, or `release/`
Keyboard ID, folder name, and display name do not contain the word "keyboard"
No `keyboard.info` file in the keyboard folder
No `docs/` or similar subfolder containing duplicate HISTORY/README/INSTALL files
No duplicate copies of the same source file inside the keyboard folder
`.kpj` file uses the current/newest project-file format
No `.kpj.user` file committed
No `build/` folder contents committed
No stray scratch or test files in the keyboard folder
If any file that ships in the package was modified, the keyboard version was bumped
If only non-package files were modified, a version bump is not required
Version number is dot-separated and entirely numeric (SemVer or CalVer)
No version component has a leading zero
Version has no `v` prefix and no non-numeric labels
New version is strictly greater than the previously deployed version
The `.kpj` file's `<Version>` field has not been bumped (it's the file-format version, not keyboard version)
`HISTORY.md` file exists
HISTORY.md header is sensible
Most recent change entry is at the top of the file
HISTORY.md is cumulative — prior entries are preserved, not overwritten
Each entry follows `<version> (<YYYY-MM-DD>)` format underlined with hyphens and bullet items
Top HISTORY.md entry version matches the version in the `.kmn` file
HISTORY.md bullets do not reference files that no longer exist in the keyboard
Copyright statement is present with year(s)
If updated across multiple years, copyright uses a year range
Copyright holder is the author or a legal entity they are authorized to claim
If an organization is named, its current legal name is used
Copyright line follows exact syntax: `Copyright (C) <year> <holder>`
Copyright year is not embedded in the `.kmn` or `.kps` copyright strings
Copyright holder name is identical across `LICENSE.md`, `.kmn`, `.kps`, `README.md`, and `HISTORY.md`
If a year range is used, it starts at the original year and extends to the current year
README.md has a good header
No version number embedded in README.md, readme.htm, welcome.htm, or help .php
No copyright year/statement in README.md, readme.htm, or welcome.htm
README.md description makes sense
README.md includes link to `https://keyman.com/keyboards/<file_name>`
README.md includes link to `http://help.keyman.com/keyboard/<file_name>`
Targets listed in README.md match those in the `.kmn`
`readme.htm` exists in `source/`
`readme.htm` content is short — just a brief description
`welcome.htm` exists in `source/`
`welcome.htm` describes how to use the keyboard
`welcome.htm` does not include instructions for installing Keyman — references help URLs instead
`<html lang="...">` attribute in `welcome.htm` matches the keyboard's primary BCP47 tag
`welcome.htm` content is not political or self-promoting
`.kmn` version matches `HISTORY.md` top entry
`.kmn` targets line uses `any` alone, not combined with individual platform names
`.kmn` targets do not include `mobile` unless a touch layout exists
Modifier key names consistent across `.kmn`, `.kvks`, and `.keyman-touch-layout`
If keyboard uses RALT, the `.kvks` includes the `usealtgr` tag
Keyboard compiles with no errors and no warnings
Keyboard display name is a readable phrase, not the underscore-separated ID
RTL flag in `.kmn`/`.kps` matches the script's actual writing direction
`store(&BITMAP)` references the actual keyboard `.ico`, not a placeholder like `qaa.ico`
All `.kmn` string-literal stores have balanced quotes and valid syntax
Author name belongs in the `.kps`, not in a `.kmn` Author store
If keyboard uses PUA codepoints, it is in `experimental/`
If keyboard uses PUA codepoints, documentation explicitly states characters/script are not in Unicode
If keyboard ships a PUA-encoded font, `.kmn` includes `store(&DISPLAYMAP)` path
OSK has been reviewed for rules, output characters, and layout consistency
The `.kvks` on-screen keyboard layout is populated, not blank
If a special font is needed for output characters, an appropriate font is selected in the OSK
Touch layout reviewed for layers, output, and fingering
If both phone and tablet touch layouts are identical, removal of one was requested
Same font is selected for phone and tablet layouts
Keyboard compiles with no errors (build-output context)
Compiled `.kmx` is listed in the `.kps` package
Compiled `.kvk` is listed in the `.kps` package
Compiled `.js` is listed if a mobile layout exists
`LICENSE.md` is listed in the `.kps` package
`readme.htm` is listed in the `.kps` package
`welcome.htm` and associated graphics are listed in the `.kps` package
All required fonts with all typefaces listed in the `.kps`
No `.php` help file listed in the `.kps` package
No files from the `help/` folder listed in the `.kps` package
No uncompiled sources (.kmn, .ico, .kvks, .keyman-touch-layout, .kps) listed in the `.kps`
No font display-map `.json` files listed in the `.kps`
No industry fonts (Arial, Times, Tahoma, etc.) in the package
Targets declared in `.kmn` match compiled files listed in the `.kps`
Every bundled font has a non-empty license field in the `.kps`
Font license identifier matches a known Open license (OFL, GPL, MIT, Apache, CC-BY, etc.)
Font license text/spirit is genuinely Open, not just labeled "freeware"
If a font is used by more than one keyboard, it lives under `shared/fonts`
Shared font path points to the current/newest shared-fonts location, not a deprecated duplicate
No `.woff` or web-only font formats in keyboard packages
If an experimental keyboard ships a PUA font, the font has been submitted to `s.keyman.com`
Font is selected in the `.kps` Keyboards tab if a font is included in Files
Language tag(s) are selected in the `.kps` Keyboards tab
Language tags are minimal — no unnecessary script/region subtags
If the tag is new or unusual, it is flagged for possible submission to langtags/Ethnologue
If keyboard is for a constructed language, it is flagged for special review
If the language tag includes numeric regional codes, it is flagged for special review
`readme.htm`, `welcome.htm`, and `LICENSE.md` are selected in the `.kps` Details tab License field
"Follow keyboard version" is selected in the `.kps` Details tab
Description field is present and non-empty in the `.kps`
Description is meaningful (becomes the keyboard home page content)
No spelling errors in user-facing `.kps` text fields (Description, Author name, WebSite text)
`<name>.php` file exists and has the proper PHP header
If using OSK syntax, all desktop and mobile layers are listed in `data-states`
All style information is inside the PHP header, not outside it
`.php` does not contain closing `</body>` or `</html>` tags
HTML in `welcome.htm` and `.php` is well-formed — balanced tags, proper headings, no unclosed elements
`.php` `data-states` attribute lists every layer defined in the `.kmn` and touch layout
`.php` `pagename` follows the standard help-site format
OSK graphics either use Keyman-generated form or custom images have width constraints
`welcome.htm` and `.php` body content are identical after stripping headers and normalizing whitespace
CSS in `welcome.htm` and `.php` are identical modulo non-rendering whitespace
BCP47 tag is correct for the language and script
No ISO 639-5 collective language codes used
No numeric regional codes unless explicitly contrastive
Region code `001` is never used in BCP47 tags
If a language uses a different script than what langtags currently records, the mismatch is flagged
A flagged script mismatch has been submitted to langtags
PUA codepoint use means keyboard is in `experimental/`
PUA codepoint use means explicit PUA notices appear in readme.htm, welcome.htm, .php, and .kps description
Keyboard does not reuse existing codepoints for unencoded scripts; if it does, warnings are in place
No mathematical Latin look-alike characters (U+1D400-U+1D7FF) in keyboard output
If math-Latin or other Latin look-alikes are present, the use case does not look like spoofing
If a third party submits a patch to an existing keyboard, the original author was consulted and approved
Original author has granted permission to relicense under MIT
New keyboard has been created in `release/` (renamed if needed)
Legacy package ID is listed in the new keyboard's `.kps` Related packages as Deprecated
A `DEPRECATED.md` file exists in the legacy keyboard's folder naming the replacement
The deprecated keyboard's help `.php` title includes "(deprecated)"
Partner package's "note for keyboard maintainers" has been followed
Keyboard version has been updated in the partner's `keyboards.csv` under `oem/`
Author has resolved all PR comments before triggering a TeamCity build
For a new author's first PR, the PR was pulled locally and checked thoroughly
After the build runs, any issues it catches that local review missed were fed back to the author
